---
title: "Multi-Model Reliability Results"
description: "Execution status and field-level diagnostics for blind multi-model annotation stress tests"
---

::: {.callout-important}
These are model-behavior diagnostics, not human inter-annotator reliability or
scholarly evidence. Consensus is not adjudication, and no result proves
reproducibility or changes an accepted annotation.
:::

This page is generated from validated case-local model-reliability artifacts by
`scripts/model_reliability/generate_results_page.py`.

**Designed but not executed:** `lincoln` has blind packets but no valid external model submissions.

## Execution status by case and layer

| Case | Task layer | State | Valid runs | Available fields | Note |
|---|---|---|---:|---:|---|
| `am-rev` | identification | absent — not designed | 0 | 0 | Multi-model reliability has not been designed for this case. |
| `am-rev` | cmt | absent — not designed | 0 | 0 | Multi-model reliability has not been designed for this case. |
| `am-rev` | interpretation | absent — not designed | 0 | 0 | Multi-model reliability has not been designed for this case. |
| `hitler` | identification | absent — not designed | 0 | 0 | Multi-model reliability has not been designed for this case. |
| `hitler` | cmt | absent — not designed | 0 | 0 | Multi-model reliability has not been designed for this case. |
| `hitler` | interpretation | absent — not designed | 0 | 0 | Multi-model reliability has not been designed for this case. |
| `lincoln` | identification | designed — not executed | 0 | 0 | Blind packets are ready; no valid external model submissions are present. |
| `lincoln` | cmt | designed — not executed | 0 | 0 | Blind packets are ready; no valid external model submissions are present. |
| `lincoln` | interpretation | designed — not executed | 0 | 0 | Blind packets are ready; no valid external model submissions are present. |
| `napoleon` | identification | absent — not designed | 0 | 0 | Multi-model reliability has not been designed for this case. |
| `napoleon` | cmt | absent — not designed | 0 | 0 | Multi-model reliability has not been designed for this case. |
| `napoleon` | interpretation | absent — not designed | 0 | 0 | Multi-model reliability has not been designed for this case. |

The same case state is repeated for each layer so missing execution cannot be
mistaken for a missing row. `Complete` means the artifact chain is valid; it
does not mean an interpretation has been proven.

## Model-to-model field stability

No validated model-to-model field results are available. This is expected while packets are designed but not executed.

Model-to-model stability asks whether separately run systems behave similarly.
Shared behavior may reflect a clear instruction, shared bias, prompt effects,
or common training data.

## Model-to-reference field divergence

No validated model-to-reference field results are available. Reference alignment has not been measured.

Reference alignment is reported separately from model-to-model stability. A
divergence may reflect model error, reference error, or codebook ambiguity; it
is a question for human review, not an automatic correction.

## Consensus, instability, and review queue

No consensus and instability report is available. Absence of a report is not evidence of agreement or reproducibility.

The review queue has human-only decision authority. Model agreement is not a
vote, and high-priority reference challenges remain unadjudicated suggestions.

## Interpretation limits

- Results remain stratified by case, source language, task layer, field, and
  run or run pair.
- Undefined and unavailable metrics stay visible rather than becoming zero or
  perfect agreement.
- Missing submissions and reports are execution states, not positive findings.
- Model outputs cannot corroborate historical claims or validate theoretical
  interpretations.
- Human source review, adjudication, and accepted-correction governance remain
  separate from this stress test.

See the [methodology](model-reliability-methodology.qmd) for the study design and
the [external review procedure](docs/reliability/external-model-review-procedures.md)
for packet execution and ingestion.
